question: A is the father of B. How is B related to A?
solution: If A is B's father, then B is A's child — son or daughter (gender not specified).

question: P is the mother of Q, and Q is a girl. How is Q related to P?
solution: Q is P's child and Q is female, so Q is P's daughter.

question: A is the brother of B, and B is the sister of C. If A, B, C are siblings, how is A related to C?
solution: A is male and a sibling of C (they share parents), so A is C's brother.

question: X is the son of Y. Y is the son of Z. How is Z related to X?
solution: X's parent is Y and Y's parent is Z, so Z is two generations above X — grandparent (grandfather, since 'son' chain).

question: A is the wife of B. How is B related to A?
solution: If A is B's wife, B is A's husband.

question: M is the daughter of N. N is the mother of O. How is M related to O (M and O are different people)?
solution: Both M and O are children of N, so they are siblings; M is female → sister.

question: A is the father of B. C is the mother of B. How is C related to A?
solution: A and C are the father and mother of the same child B, so they are husband and wife; C is female → wife.
trick: Parents of the same child = spouses.

question: P is the brother of Q. Q is the father of R. How is P related to R?
solution: P is R's father's brother, i.e. R's uncle.

question: A is the son of B. C is the sister of A. How is C related to B?
solution: A and C are siblings, both children of B; C is female → B's daughter.
trick: Sibling of a child = another child.

question: Pointing to a boy, Reena said, 'He is the son of my mother.' How is the boy related to Reena?
solution: 'Son of my mother' is Reena's brother.

question: A is the mother of B and C. D is the husband of A. How is D related to C?
solution: A is C's mother; D is A's husband, so D is C's father.

question: X is the daughter of Y. Z is the husband of X. How is Y related to Z?
solution: X is Y's daughter and Z is X's husband, so Y is Z's parent-in-law (father- or mother-in-law).

question: Pointing to a photo, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is she related to the man?
solution: Grandfather's only son is the man's father; his daughter is the man's sister.

question: A is the father of B. B is the mother of C. How is A related to C?
solution: A is B's father and B is C's parent, so A is C's grandfather.
trick: Parent of a parent = grandparent (male → grandfather).

question: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
solution: 'Only daughter of my mother' is the woman herself; so the man's mother is the woman → she is his mother.
trick: 'Only daughter of my mother' = the speaker herself.

question: A + B means A is the mother of B; A − B means A is the brother of B. In P + Q − R, how is P related to R?
solution: P is mother of Q, and Q is brother of R, so Q and R are siblings — both children of P; P is R's mother.
trick: Mother of one sibling = mother of all.

question: A is B's father's sister's husband. How is A related to B?
solution: Father's sister is B's aunt; her husband is B's uncle.

question: Pointing to a lady, Ravi said, 'She is the mother of the son of my wife.' How is the lady related to Ravi?
solution: 'Son of my wife' is Ravi's son; the son's mother is Ravi's wife.
trick: Mother of your son = your wife.

question: A is the son of C. C and D are sisters. E is the mother of D. How is E related to A?
solution: C and D are sisters, so E (D's mother) is also C's mother; A is C's son, so E is A's grandmother.

question: If P is the brother of Q, R is the mother of Q, S is the father of R, then how is S related to P?
solution: R is Q's (and P's) mother; S is R's father, so S is P's maternal grandfather.
